claim: Britain's output per person has changed materially since 2007
caveat: GDP per head rose 7.7% from £37,625 (2007) to £40,537 (2025). Growth averaged ~0.4% per year.

claim: National income per head has changed materially since 2007
caveat: NDP per head rose 3.7%, less than half the rate of GDP per head. Suggests capital consumption absorbed a growing share of output.

claim: Productivity growth has been weak since 2007
caveat: Output per hour rose 6.9% over 18 years (~0.4% pa), compared with ~2% pa pre-2007. Clear evidence of productivity stagnation.
Mixed picture: 7 regions gained relative to UK average, 5 declined. Scotland (+7.3%) and NI (+8.1%) converged; London (−7.3%) and East of England (−2.8%) saw the largest relative falls.

expected_output: London vs UK average and regions
caveat: London output per hour: 129 (UK=100) vs 139 in 2007. Remains the clear outlier but the gap has narrowed.

claim: Living standards changed differently from GDP per head
expected_output: Comparison with earnings or income measure
caveat: Real earnings/household income indicator not yet populated in this POC.
